summary:
The presence of anosmia or dysgeusia is a hallmark clinical distinction between COVID-19 and influenza, occurring in up to 70% of early-stage COVID-19 cases versus rarity in influenza. This symptom, combined with rapid molecular diagnostics, aids clinicians in differentiating the two diseases even when their overlapping features suggest otherwise.  

\boxed{A}
